name: Roasted Chicken Breasts with Cranberry Port Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. To purchase boneless chicken breasts with the skin on, buy chicken breasts on the bone and ask your butcher or supermarket to remove the bones but leave the skin intact. This presentation is more beautiful and keeps the chicken moist. Bones can be used for stock or discarded. If you prefer skinless chicken, make a cut along the bottom of each breast to form a pocket. Stuff each breast with an equal amount of filling and turn the slit side down. Brush the top with a film of Dijon mustard and proceed with the recipe.
2. To enhance mushroom flavor, add one package of soaked dried mushrooms along with regular mushrooms if desired. Fresh cranberries can be substituted for dried cranberries.
3. Trim chicken breasts of any fat. In a skillet over medium-high heat, melt butter. Add onion and sauté until softened. Add mushrooms and zucchini, stirring occasionally, until all liquid has evaporated (about 10 minutes). Stir in tarragon and cream, continuing to cook until the cream reduces and the mixture is thick (about 5 minutes).
4. Stir in lemon juice and add enough breadcrumbs to hold the mixture together. Mix in parsley and season well with salt and pepper.
5. Season chicken breasts with salt and pepper. Using your fingertips, make a pocket between the skin and flesh of each breast. Stuff about one-third cup of filling under the skin, tucking the skin around the breast to enclose the stuffing.
6. Place chicken breasts in a large oiled (preferably metal) baking dish or roasting pan. Dot with butter and refrigerate for at least one hour before baking.
7. Bake in a 400°F (200°C) oven for 30 to 40 minutes, or until juices run clear. If the skin is not crisp enough, place under the broiler to crisp it.
8. While baking chicken, bring sauce ingredients to a boil in a pot over medium-high heat. Reduce by half, skimming occasionally, for about 25 minutes.
9. Over low heat, whisk in butter one tablespoon at a time until the sauce is slightly thickened and glossy. Just before serving, skim any fat from pan juices and add them to the sauce.
10. Serve chicken breasts sliced into 1/4 inch slices and coat with sauce, or serve whole with sauce in a sauceboat. Garnish with extra dried cranberries if desired.
